_doc: Per-family row-count gate for the v17.1 build pipeline. Per tmpl_gen/templates/05102026/v17_1_plan.txt §2. v17.1 is a CHAINED CyberSOCEval-letter-set specialist shard built off v16; the Sophia-CTI-Templates-v17.1.txt manifest declares the SAME 14 JS.CSE.* families as v17, with `Shuffle: mcq_multi` added to every template (data-fix recovery; no Count: changes vs v17). Thresholds carried verbatim from tmpl_gen/templates/05102026/v17_row_count_gate.json since the Count: budget is unchanged and the engine-side Shuffle directive is post-render (it does not affect row counts). v17 actuals against this same plan: CSE-TI-actor 5914 / CSE-TI-other 2242 / CSE-MAL-rpt 5039 / CSE-MAL-other 3753 (all OK). v17.1 should land in the same neighbourhood within stochastic noise.

axes._note: JS.CSE.TI.GRP.{1,2,3} + JS.CSE.TI.MAL.{1,2,3} declared sum: 1500*3 + 2500 + 1500*2 = 10000. Floor sized to ~50% of declared to give the per-actor cap (60) headroom. v17 actual under this plan: 5914 (above 5000 floor, below 8000 target -- expected because per-actor cap engages on well-documented intrusion sets like APT29).

JS.CSE.TI.CMP.1 declared 2500 against the ~52-campaign anchor pool; under per_primary_grouping=true the engine balances across the limited campaign set. JS.CSE.TI.NEG.1 declared 1500 with Per_primary_grouping: false. v17 actual under this plan: 2242 (above 1400 floor, slightly above 2200 target).
JS.CSE.MAL.RPT.{1,2,3} declared sum: 2500 + 1500 + 1500 = 5500 against the ~830 malware anchor pool. v17 actual under this plan: 5039 (above 3000 floor, above 4500 target).

JS.CSE.MAL.TAC.1 declared 1500 against the 14-tactic anchor pool. JS.CSE.MAL.TGT.1 declared 1500 (single-correct group attribution). JS.CSE.MAL.NEG.1 declared 1000 with Per_primary_grouping: false. v17 actual under this plan: 3753 (above 2000 floor, above 3300 target).
